claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined for each child individually.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, how old are they?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children I'm responsible for. I have two kids - Alex who is 5 years old, and Blake who is 10. They both live at home with me. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you have Alex (5 years old) and Blake (10 years old) living at home with you.

Since both Alex and Blake are under 16, they potentially qualify as children for Child Benefit purposes. Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Are you the person responsible for Alex and Blake?** By this I mean, do you live with them and are you responsible for their care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I live with both Alex and Blake, and I'm responsible for their care. They live at home with me full-time and I look after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand a bit more about your immigration and residency status:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK?** This could mean you're:
- A British citizen
- An Irish citizen
- Have settled status
- Have pre-settled status
- Have another immigration status that gives you the right to reside and access benefits

What is your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen, so yes, I have the right to reside in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Now I need to ask about whether anyone else might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Blake:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?** For example, another parent, guardian, or family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them and nobody else is getting any benefits for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are Alex and Blake normally living with you?** By "normally," I mean do they live with you most of the time, even if they might occasionally stay elsewhere (like with another parent for visits, or at a relative's house)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they normally live with me. They're at home with me all the time - this is their main home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to ask about any circumstances that might affect their living situation:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in care (meaning they are being looked after by the local authority), or are they in hospital or a residential home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in care or in hospital or anything like that. They're just at home with me as normal.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just ask one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is there anyone else who has an equal or greater claim to Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?** For example, is there another parent or person who contributes equally or more to their care, or with whom they spend equal or more time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else with an equal or greater claim. I'm the one who looks after them and they live with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Let me review the information you've provided to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:

**For Alex (5 years old):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Lives with you and you're responsible for their care ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You're a British citizen with the right to reside ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them ✓
- They normally live with you ✓
- Not in care or hospital ✓
- No one else has an equal or greater claim ✓

**For Blake (10 years old):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Lives with you and you're responsible for their care ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You're a British citizen with the right to reside ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them ✓
- They normally live with you ✓
- Not in care or hospital ✓
- No one else has an equal or greater claim ✓

**Good news! You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

You meet all the qualifying conditions:
- Both children are under 16
- You are responsible for them and they live with you
- You have the right to reside in the UK as a British citizen
- No one else is claiming for them or has a greater claim

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. You'll receive a payment for each child - the rate is higher for the first child and a different rate for additional children.

**Please note:** If you or your partner has an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Would you like any information about how to make your claim?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much for explaining all of that! I really appreciate you going through everything with me. I think I have all the information I need for now. Thanks again for your help!
